title: Contribution Margin Analysis: If a product sells for 80 and has variable costs...
summary: Calculate the contribution margin per unit as selling price minus variable cost per unit.
prompt: If a product sells for $80 and has variable costs of $30, what is the contribution margin per unit?
solution: Contribution Margin per Unit = Selling Price - Variable Cost = $80 - $30 = $50. This is essential for understanding how much each unit sold contributes to covering fixed costs (Page 47).
